summary: Namespace isolation remains closed, but the hosted<->delegated shared-vault contract still breaks for an allowed same-org cross-user bridge path.

findings.title: Same-org cross-user bridge delegation still derives a different vault than the hosted conversation owner
findings.recommendation: The provider only checks that the conversation belongs to the pairing org, then calls the chokepoint with `pairing.ownerUserId`. Hosted SDK turns key user_work vaults as `csid:<session owner>:<sessionId>`, while delegated turns key as `csid:<pairing owner>:<sessionId>`. For a same-org delegation where those users differ, hosted-minted tokens still cannot detokenize delegated. Either reject non-owner sessions here or resolve/pass the session owner as the vault principal while preserving the intended billing owner separately, then add a regression where session owner != pairing owner and a delegated response contains a hosted-minted token.
